properties.name: Yirgacheffe
properties.description: Ethiopia's most celebrated coffee origin, Yirgacheffe produces some of the world's most distinctive coffees. High altitude, lush vegetation, and generations of traditional farming produce coffees with extraordinary florality, delicate tea-like qualities, and bright citrus acidity.

properties.name: Sidama
properties.description: Adjacent to Yirgacheffe, Sidama produces coffees of similar prestige. Natural processing here creates extraordinarily fruity and syrupy cups, while washed lots exhibit clean, floral complexity. The region's indigenous tree varieties are genetically unique.

properties.name: Guji
properties.description: An emerging specialty powerhouse adjacent to Sidama and Yirgacheffe, Guji produces coffees with wild, fruity naturals and complex washed lots with bright stone-fruit acidity. Heirloom varieties grown by smallholders at very high altitudes.

properties.name: Harrar
properties.description: One of Ethiopia's oldest known coffee origins, Harrar (or Harar) is famous for bold, natural-processed coffees with distinctive blueberry and wine-like notes. The dry, semi-arid plateau produces unique, rustic character unlike anywhere else in Ethiopia.

properties.name: Limu
properties.description: Limu is a fertile, well-watered region producing mild, washed coffees with distinct spicy and wine-like character. One of Ethiopia's most exported commercial origins, with a growing reputation for specialty lots from smallholder cooperatives.

properties.name: Gimbi / Wollega
properties.description: Located in western Ethiopia, the Gimbi/Wollega region produces bold, full-bodied coffees with earthy spice notes. One of Ethiopia's highest-volume exporting regions, known for both commercial washed lots and increasingly prized naturals.

properties.name: Nyeri
properties.description: Considered the crown jewel of Kenyan coffee, Nyeri sits on the slopes of Mt Kenya and the Aberdare Range. Exceptional red and volcanic soil, cool temperatures, and the double-washing Kenya wet process combine to produce coffees of extraordinary acidity, body, and blackcurrant intensity.

properties.name: Kirinyaga
properties.description: Kirinyaga county wraps around the southern slopes of Mt Kenya, its fertile volcanic soils and cool, mist-laden mornings delivering dense, complex beans. A consistently outstanding region for Kenya AA grade coffee.

properties.name: Kiambu
properties.description: Kiambu is the southernmost major Kenyan coffee region, just north of Nairobi. While cooler temperatures moderate acidity slightly, the region still produces classic Kenya profiles with deep berry fruit and complex body.

properties.name: Tarime
properties.description: Tanzania's Tarime district in the Mara region, near Lake Victoria, is an emerging specialty origin. Volcanic soils and high humidity produce coffees with distinctive tropical fruit character, especially when naturally processed.

properties.name: Nyamasheke
properties.description: On the western edge of Rwanda bordering Lake Kivu and the DRC, Nyamasheke's misty, high-altitude hills produce coffees of exceptional vibrancy and red fruit character. Red Bourbon thrives here under traditional shade systems.

properties.name: Nyamagabe
properties.description: Central Rwanda's Nyamagabe district is one of the country's finest specialty origins. High altitude, rich soils, and a diligent washing station network produce some of Rwanda's most complex and highly scored lots.

properties.name: Kayanza
properties.description: Kayanza in northern Burundi is the country's most prestigious coffee province. Extreme altitude and fertile soils produce high-density beans with intense fruit, deep body, and complex sweetness. The terroir here rivals the finest East African origins.

properties.name: Gitega
properties.description: Central Burundi's Gitega province is gaining recognition for its complex, elegant washed coffees. The unique geology and altitude create distinct terroir character with vibrant berry fruit and clean acidity.

properties.name: Bugisu
properties.description: Mount Elgon's Bugisu region is Uganda's most celebrated coffee-producing area. The fertile volcanic slopes of the dormant volcano provide ideal conditions for clean, complex washed Arabica with distinctive brightness.

properties.name: Huila
properties.description: Huila is Colombia's most celebrated specialty producing department, delivering an extraordinary range of profiles from bright, acidic washed lots to syrupy, complex naturals and experimentals. Home to thousands of smallholder farms in high-altitude valleys.

properties.name: Nariño
properties.description: Nariño is Colombia's southernmost major coffee region, bordering Ecuador. The extreme altitude and proximity to the equator allows for very slow cherry maturation, developing extraordinary sugar content and complex citric acidity.

properties.name: Antioquia
properties.description: Antioquia is Colombia's most productive coffee department by volume. The famous 'Eje Cafetero' region spans Antioquia, Caldas, and Risaralda. Rich volcanic soils and moderate altitude produce approachable, balanced coffees that form the backbone of many blends.

properties.name: Cauca
properties.description: Cauca is an emerging specialty origin in Colombia's Pacific corridor. High-altitude farms here produce exceptionally sweet, balanced coffees with bright fruit and clean acidity. A region of increasing interest to specialty buyers.

properties.name: Cundinamarca
properties.description: Cundinamarca surrounds Bogotá, Colombia's capital. A historically important but now niche origin, it is home to innovative farms like La Palma y El Tucán who are pioneering experimental processing and rare varieties.

properties.name: Sul de Minas
properties.description: Sul de Minas is Brazil's most important specialty producing sub-region. Located in the southern highlands of Minas Gerais, its red latosol soils and mild climate produce consistently sweet, full-bodied coffees that form the foundation of espresso blends worldwide.

properties.name: Cerrado Mineiro
properties.description: Cerrado Mineiro in northwest Minas Gerais was the first Brazilian coffee region to receive a geographical indication. Its savannah climate with a defined dry season allows for machine harvesting and consistent, full-bodied naturals.

properties.name: Espírito Santo
properties.description: The most important Robusta-producing state in Brazil, Espírito Santo's higher-altitude areas also produce quality Arabica, including the unique Caparaó and Baixadão origins known for natural process brightness.

properties.name: San Martín
properties.description: San Martín in northern Peru's Amazon region is one of Peru's most productive specialty origins. Smallholder cooperatives here produce clean, bright washed coffees with delicate stone fruit character.

properties.name: Ayacucho
properties.description: A remote, high-altitude region in the central Peruvian Andes. Difficult terrain limits production but concentrates flavour. Cooperative farmers here are beginning to attract serious specialty attention for their delicate, sweet washed lots.

properties.name: Amazonas
properties.description: The remote Amazonas department in northern Peru borders Ecuador. Home to biodiverse cloud forest ecosystems, it produces low-volume, shade-grown coffees of increasing specialty interest. The Amigos del Oso cooperative protects spectacled bear habitat.

properties.name: Huehuetenango
properties.description: Huehuetenango is Guatemala's most prestigious coffee-producing region, isolated by mountains and warmed by hot dry winds (surazos) from the Tehuantepec plain. This unique microclimate allows coffee to thrive at very high altitudes producing complex, full-bodied coffees.

properties.name: Atitlán
properties.description: Surrounding the spectacular volcanic crater lake, Atitlán's volcanic soils and unique microclimate produce balanced, sweet coffees. Three surrounding volcanoes create a diverse range of microclimates.

properties.name: Copán
properties.description: Copán in western Honduras, bordering Guatemala, is one of the country's original specialty zones. The Copán archaeological zone nearby brings tourism that supports coffee communities. Lush cloud forest provides ideal shade-grown conditions.

properties.name: Tarrazú
properties.description: Tarrazú is Costa Rica's most internationally recognised coffee region. Located in the mountains south of San José, its combination of volcanic soil, high altitude, and micro-mill honey process innovation has produced a steady stream of world-class coffees.

properties.name: Boquete
properties.description: A small but extraordinarily prestigious valley on the slopes of Volcán Barú, Boquete is home to Panama's most acclaimed estates including Hacienda La Esmeralda and Elida Estate. The combination of volcanic soil, cloud cover, and elevation creates near-perfect conditions for Gesha.

properties.name: Volcán
properties.description: The higher slopes on the western face of Volcán Barú, Volcán is home to pioneering estates like Finca Deborah. Even more extreme altitude than Boquete, producing intensely concentrated and aromatic coffees.

properties.name: Apaneca-Ilamatepec
properties.description: The Apaneca-Ilamatepec mountain range in western El Salvador includes the Apaneca highlands between two volcanoes. The region consistently produces El Salvador's highest-scoring competition coffees, particularly Pacamara.

properties.name: Santa Ana
properties.description: Santa Ana in western El Salvador is home to shade-grown Bourbon grown on the slopes of the Santa Ana volcano. Aida Batlle's farms here helped put El Salvador back on the specialty map after decades of focusing on commercial production.

properties.name: Jinotega
properties.description: Jinotega is Nicaragua's most important coffee department, accounting for the majority of production. High altitude mountain ranges and fertile volcanic soil produce coffees of surprising complexity for the region.

properties.name: Oaxaca
properties.description: Oaxaca in southern Mexico is home to indigenous communities who have cultivated shade-grown coffee for generations. Typica and Bourbon varieties produce mild, sweet coffees with distinct chocolate and nut character.

properties.name: Malabar Coast
properties.description: The Malabar Coast of Karnataka is the spiritual home of Monsoon Malabar — one of the world's most unique coffees. Beans are exposed to monsoon winds for 12–16 weeks, swelling to almost twice their size and developing a distinctive low-acid, heavy-bodied, earthy character.
